topic: RAR lensing extension — adversarial review of EXP-407 horizon-analogy preemption paragraph
our_argument_summary: WIN-052 verdict 'Self-Contradicted' (data/wins.json:WIN-052:verdict). Detail body argues: dome's 20,015 km / 9,086 km bounded firmament cannot accommodate 1–100 kpc RAR galaxies; ρ_A=g₀ρ_bar/g† formula relabels MOND without derivation; false-dilemma critique (now subordinated to structural-incoherence per ISS-2027 fix); λ_A=4,283 Mpc = c/H₀ to 0.01% promoted to tldr_verdict (ISS-2028 fix); fails post-hoc test (no monitor.py entry, value adopted from published SPARC/Mistele datasets). NEW THIS CYCLE: EXP-407 horizon-analogy preemption paragraph added between MOND-priority paragraph and post-hoc-test paragraph in detail_verdict_text (~343 new words). Three-pronged preemption: (1) positive-claims-not-silence (unified-theory CONSISTENT entry + RAR-as-strongest-confirmation), (2) mechanism-emptiness (cosmic horizons presuppose Hubble flow which dome rejects), (3) same-parameter-double-duty (firmament_height invoked as hard ELF reflector in WIN-029 and hard magnetic cap in WIN-053). Closes ISS-2026, partially ISS-2028.

dome_code_analysis.note: Code-tag re-validation deferred per c4/c5/c6 precedent. WIN-052 has monitoring=none and no automated calculation; this cycle is an adversarial review of new prose, not a re-derivation of code tags.
dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: No automated calculation. monitoring=none. Carried forward from c6.
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: None this cycle. Code-tag findings (monitoring=none, post_hoc=true, derives_from_dome=false) unchanged from c6.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: Carried forward from c2/c3/c4/c5/c6. data/wins.json:WIN-052:code_analysis.monitoring="none" verified verbatim this run on clone HEAD cd9e9cc9. EXP-407 added prose only; no automation introduced.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: Carried forward. RAR is an empirical regularity (not standard physics being relabelled). Tag correctly false. The MOND-relabel critique lives in the prose body, not in this tag.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: Carried forward. McGaugh+ 2016 PRL and Mistele+ 2024 ApJL both predate V51.0 import. detail_verdict_text closing paragraph (data/wins.json:WIN-052:detail_verdict_text) explicitly applies the post-hoc test and concludes the legitimate-novelty carve-out does not apply.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: Carried forward. No derivation chain from dome parameters (disc_radius=20,015 km, firmament_height=9,086 km) to RAR-relevant kpc scales. EXP-407 strengthens this finding — prong 2 (mechanism-emptiness) and the EXP-407 anticipated-objection responses make the no-derivation point explicit: defender's retreat from λ_A=4,283 Mpc as a 'spatial-extent claim' to 'merely a wave-equation parameter' is unsupported because the dome publishes no derivation either way.

holes_found.description: PRONG 2 FACTUAL ERROR — 'particle horizon' is conflated with 'Hubble horizon'. The new paragraph asserts: 'Standard cosmology\'s particle horizon is a recession-velocity boundary at the distance where Hubble flow exceeds c.' This conflates two distinct standard-cosmology concepts: (a) the **particle horizon**, defined by lookback integral c·∫₀^t₀ dt'/a(t') — i.e., the comoving distance light could have traveled since the Big Bang (~46 Gly in ΛCDM today); and (b) the **Hubble horizon** (or Hubble sphere/Hubble radius), defined as c/H — the distance at which recession velocity equals c (~14.4 Gly in ΛCDM today). It is the Hubble horizon, not the particle horizon, that is a 'recession-velocity boundary at the distance where Hubble flow exceeds c'. A cosmologist defender (or any astrophysics-trained reader) will identify this as a textbook factual error in a critique that elsewhere makes precision claims to 0.01%. The error undercuts prong 2's authority even if the underlying mechanism-emptiness argument is sound. ANCHORING: data/wins.json:WIN-052:detail_verdict_text contains the verbatim string 'Standard cosmology\'s particle horizon is a recession-velocity boundary at the distance where Hubble flow exceeds c' (verified this run on clone HEAD cd9e9cc9).
holes_found.recommendation: Single-string replacement in data/wins.json:WIN-052:detail_verdict_text. Replace 'Standard cosmology\'s particle horizon is a recession-velocity boundary at the distance where Hubble flow exceeds c; it presupposes Hubble expansion.' with 'Standard cosmology\'s Hubble horizon is a recession-velocity boundary at the distance where Hubble flow exceeds c; it presupposes Hubble expansion.' Alternative: use 'cosmological horizons' (plural, generic) to sidestep the specific-term taxonomy. Either way, 'particle horizon' is the wrong term for the recession-velocity boundary. Severity moderate because (a) the error is small in word-count but salient (any reader who notices it discredits the prong), and (b) the fix is one-token.
holes_found.defender_payoff: A working cosmologist reading our review would tweet: 'They critique a flat-earth model but conflate particle horizon with Hubble horizon in a paragraph attacking the dome\'s misuse of horizon terminology. Pot, kettle.' The reputational damage is the kind of paper-cut a careful reviewer cannot afford.

holes_found.description: PRONG 3 CITATION ACCURACY — WIN-053 does not actually treat firmament_height=9,086 km as a 'hard magnetic-cap boundary'. The new paragraph asserts: 'The model invokes firmament_height = 9,086 km ... as a hard magnetic-cap boundary in WIN-053'. Cross-check of data/wins.json:WIN-053:detail_evidence (read this run from clone HEAD cd9e9cc9) shows WIN-053's argument is about (a) the closed toroidal ovoid topology (north pole source + sub-terrestrial Sump return), (b) flux-conservation requiring B_south≈39 nT vs. fitted 64,852 nT (1,660:1 self-contradiction), and (c) the SHARED scale length λ=8,619 km appearing in both H(r) firmament-height and B(r) magnetic-field equations — a 'single parameter pressed into service for two structurally unrelated physical phenomena'. WIN-053 does NOT argue from firmament_height = 9,086 km as a hard magnetic ceiling/cap. The phrase 'firmament_height = 9,086 km' appears in WIN-053:detail_evidence only as an example dome geometric property explicitly NOT derivationally connected to B(r): 'No derivation from dome parameters (disc_radius, firmament_height, or any geometric property) is provided'. So prong 3 mischaracterizes our own WIN-053 — a defender who reads our WIN-053 carefully will say: 'Your own review acknowledges firmament_height does NOT derivationally appear in B(r); your prong 3 then asserts the opposite. Pick a story.'
holes_found.recommendation: Two-option fix. (a) Re-phrase prong 3's WIN-053 citation to point at the SHARED-PARAMETER critique that is actually in WIN-053: replace 'and as a hard magnetic-cap boundary in WIN-053' with 'and as a co-opted scale-length in WIN-053\'s toroidal-ovoid magnetic field, where the same λ=8,619 km governs both firmament-height H(r) and magnetic-field B(r)'. This preserves the 'same parameter in unrelated phenomena' structural critique. (b) Drop the WIN-053 reference entirely and cite a different WIN where firmament_height IS treated as a hard EM/magnetic boundary (e.g., re-purpose WIN-029 or find a Schumann-companion WIN). Option (a) is preferred because it is closer to the spirit of prong 3 (same structural parameter pulling double duty). Severity moderate because the prong's spirit holds (parameter double-duty IS a real dome problem) but the specific WIN-053 anchoring is wrong and a careful defender will notice.
holes_found.defender_payoff: Defender reply: 'Your prong 3 invokes WIN-053 as treating firmament_height as a hard magnetic cap. WIN-053\'s own detail_evidence explicitly notes no derivation from firmament_height to B(r). You\'re inventing a structural identity your own review does not assert.' If we want this prong to hit, the citation must match what WIN-053 actually says.

holes_found.description: PRONG 3 FORCED-DICHOTOMY HAS AN ESCAPE THE PARAGRAPH DOES NOT FORECLOSE. EXP-407 anticipated_objections[2] explicitly identifies a defender's pivot: 'the dome could in principle say firmament_height is BOTH a hard ELF reflector AND a soft galactic-scale horizon if the two regimes are governed by different sectors of the model (one electromagnetic, one gravitational).' EXP-407 has a response: 'That defense would require the dome to publish the two-sector decomposition... The dome publishes one parameter table with one firmament_height value and invokes it for both phenomena.' But the live paragraph as written contains the prong's assertion (firmament can\'t be soft for galaxies and hard for Schumann) and the closing forced-choice (retract-or-concede) — neither of which mentions or forecloses the sector-decomposition escape. The forced choice at paragraph-end is framed around the unified-theory CONSISTENT entry (prong 1's anchor), not around prong 3\'s same-parameter-double-duty critique. So a defender can pivot to 'EM and gravitational sectors with the same numerical firmament_height value' without retracting the unified-theory entry. The paragraph\'s defense survives this pivot only via the unwritten EXP-407 anticipated-objection response — which is not in the prose readers see.
holes_found.recommendation: Append a single sentence to prong 3 (within the same paragraph) closing the sector-decomposition escape. Suggested text after 'where the model needs deniability': ' — and partitioning the parameter\'s electromagnetic and gravitational roles into separate model sectors would require the dome to publish the two-sector derivation it does not have.' Net add ~22 words. This lands the EXP-407 anticipated-objection response in the live text where defenders can see it. Severity moderate because the gap is documented (EXP-407 knew about it) but the response was not propagated into the prose.
holes_found.defender_payoff: Defender: 'Prong 3 forces a false dichotomy. The dome can have two parameter-roles (EM sector and gravitational sector) at the same numerical value of firmament_height. The Schumann use is electromagnetic; the galactic use is gravitational. Same number, different physics sectors — no contradiction.' Without the anticipated-objection response in the prose, this defender pivot is not foreclosed.

holes_found.description: PRONG 1 ELIDES ρ_A (DENSITY) vs λ_A (LENGTH). The setup sentence references 'λ_A = 4,283 Mpc reframed as merely a wave-equation parameter' — setting up λ_A as the cosmological-scale claim. Prong 1 then introduces 'ρ_A = g_0 ρ_bar / g†' (a density relation) and concludes 'Cosmological-scale parameter plus cosmological-scale empirical confirmation is not agnosticism' — but the parameter referenced in prong 1 is ρ_A (a density, units kg/m³), not λ_A (a length, units Mpc). The 'cosmological-scale parameter' qualifier inside prong 1 leans on the setup\'s λ_A reference rather than on the ρ_A in-prong claim. A careful defender could say: 'ρ_A is a density relation that the dome could in principle apply only within the disc — it doesn\'t intrinsically carry a galactic length-scale; you\'re piggybacking on the setup\'s λ_A reference to make this prong work.' The fix is to either (a) name λ_A inside prong 1, or (b) state that ρ_A is applied to RAR data spanning 1–100 kpc galaxy scales, which is what makes ρ_A's empirical claim cosmological-scale.
holes_found.recommendation: Optional single-clause edit. Within prong 1, append after 'as its strongest cosmological confirmation': '— and the RAR data the dome cites span galaxy scales of 1–100 kpc, locking the ρ_A claim to those scales by its empirical anchor'. This nails down the cosmological-scale qualifier without relying on the setup\'s λ_A reference. Severity minor because the prong's argument structure still holds for a sympathetic reader; only a defender who pulls apart the density/length distinction will notice.

holes_found.description: CLOSING DICHOTOMY HAS A THIRD MOVE (REINTERPRETATION). 'The defender must choose: retract... or keep them and concede positive cosmological-scale claims without a derivation. Either move concedes the Self-Contradicted verdict.' A clever defender has a third option: REINTERPRET the unified-theory CONSISTENT entry as 'structurally compatible with the dome framework when read as a within-disc relation' and reframe 'strongest cosmological confirmation' as 'the dome\'s relation captures the same phenomenology the RAR exhibits, whatever the underlying interpretation'. Neither retraction nor concession of cosmological-scale claims — it\'s a content-draining reinterpretation. The reinterpretation move is Pyrrhic (it costs the original confident framing of WIN-052) but it sidesteps the forced-choice as stated.
holes_found.recommendation: Optional addition to the closing sentence. After 'or keep them and concede positive cosmological-scale claims without a derivation' add ' — or reinterpret CONSISTENT as compatible-when-restricted-to-within-disc, which silently retracts the WIN\'s cosmological-scale claim while pretending not to'. Severity minor — the existing dichotomy is rhetorically strong and any reinterpretation route is itself a partial retraction the dome would not advertise.
holes_found.defender_payoff: A philosophically-inclined defender argues: 'You\'re forcing a false dichotomy. We can keep the entries and reinterpret their scope.' This works rhetorically until pushed. So it\'s a defender talking-point but not a winning move.

holes_found.description: WIN-029 CROSS-CITATION SLIGHTLY OVERSTATES — WIN-029:detail_evidence flags an internal inconsistency in the dome\'s own firmament-height values: 'H(r=0) = 8,537 km from its own exponential formula, vs. firmament_height = 9,086 km from the model\'s stated core parameters — a 6.4% discrepancy never acknowledged'. The new paragraph asserts 'firmament_height = 9,086 km' as if it were unambiguous, while our own WIN-029 documents the dome publishing two inconsistent values. A pedantic defender could reply: 'You\'re asserting firmament_height = 9,086 km confidently in the WIN-052 horizon paragraph, while in WIN-029 you flag that the dome itself publishes 9,086 vs 8,537. Pick one.' This is a minor self-consistency wrinkle — not a fatal flaw because the WIN-052 paragraph is critiquing the 9,086 value the dome publishes as its 'stated core parameter', and the 8,537/9,086 mismatch is a separate (additional) dome inconsistency.
holes_found.recommendation: Optional one-word qualifier within prong 3. Replace 'firmament_height = 9,086 km' with 'the dome\'s published firmament_height value (9,086 km in the model\'s stated core parameters; cf. WIN-029 on the internal 9,086/8,537 inconsistency)' — or simpler: leave as-is and accept the minor wrinkle. Severity minor.

stronger_arguments.description: LEVERAGE THE NUMERICAL MATCH λ_A=4,283 Mpc ≈ c/H₀ TO 0.01% INSIDE PRONG 2. The WIN\'s tldr_verdict and detail_extra already feature this numerical near-identity. Prong 2 (mechanism-emptiness) currently says 'λ_A is a fitted length-scale, not a recession-velocity scale' — but the defender can reply 'fitted to RAR data, fine'. The crushing reply is that RAR data spans 1–100 kpc (six orders of magnitude smaller than 4,283 Mpc); no fit to RAR data alone produces a 4,283 Mpc length scale matching c/H₀ to 0.01%. Either the match is a 1-in-10⁴ coincidence, or the dome is implicitly importing the Hubble length while denying the Hubble mechanism. Adding one sentence to prong 2 — 'The numerical near-identity λ_A≈c/H₀ to 0.01% precision is not derivable from a fit to 1–100 kpc galaxy data; it is the Hubble length wearing a different label — which means the dome is implicitly importing the Hubble physics it pretends to reject' — would make prong 2 substantially harder to escape.
sources: data/wins.json:WIN-052:tldr_verdict — already contains 'λ_A = 4,283 Mpc — c/H₀ to 0.01% precision'
data/wins.json:WIN-052:detail_extra — cross-WIN globe-fingerprint paragraph on λ_A

stronger_arguments.description: PRONG 2 SELF-DEFEAT LEVER. If a defender retreats to 'λ_A is merely a wave-equation parameter' (the setup line), they are simultaneously retreating from WIN-052\'s headline claim that the aetheric medium EXPLAINS the RAR. WIN-052\'s detail_claim says: 'the dome claims its aetheric medium explains this better than particle dark matter — citing Mistele et al. (2024) as its strongest cosmological confirmation'. If λ_A is 'merely a wave-equation parameter' with no cosmological-scale commitment, the aetheric medium\'s claim to explain the RAR collapses. So the horizon-analogy retreat path SELF-DEFEATS the WIN it is supposed to save. Prong 2 could explicitly name this: the retreat to 'merely a wave-equation parameter' costs the defender the WIN-052 claim they are attempting to defend, before any further argument.

EXP-407 ANTICIPATED-OBJECTIONS RESPONSES SHOULD BE INLINED, NOT PARKED. EXP-407 enumerates three anticipated objections from defenders (the 'CONSISTENT is just a label', 'analogies do not import full machinery', 'different sectors of the model') and provides responses. Two of those responses are not in the live paragraph. The 'different sectors' response is Hole 3 above. The 'analogies do not import full machinery' response is similarly absent from the live prose: prong 2 baldly asserts 'an analogy that depends on the premise of the theory it opposes is empty' without explaining why a defender\'s 'just structural, not mechanistic' interpretation fails. EXP-407\'s response — 'the moment the analogy sheds the mechanism, it sheds the legitimacy and reduces to we have a hard wall and won\'t talk about what is outside it, which is the contradiction' — is the load-bearing rebuttal. The paragraph would be substantively stronger by inlining a one-clause version of this rebuttal into prong 2.

kernel_of_truth.description: The three-pronged structure (positive-claims-not-silence, mechanism-emptiness, same-parameter-double-duty) is the correct decomposition of the horizon-analogy attack space — each prong attacks an independent leg of the defender's potential retreat, and EXP-407\'s author (monitor/analyst/expansions/EXP-407-WIN-052-horizon-analogy-preemption.json:review_class_rationale) correctly identified each as a separate line of attack that does not refine an existing argument. The paragraph also lands the load-bearing rhetorical move: a forced choice between silence (which weakens the WIN being defended) and concession (which concedes the Self-Contradicted verdict). The kernel of truth in the defender\'s horizon analogy — that cosmological theories DO have observational boundaries (EXP-407:kernel_of_truth) — is acknowledged in the EXP package and engaged head-on rather than dismissed.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: The structural correctness of the three-prong decomposition does not save the live paragraph from its specific execution problems (Holes 1–3 above). Prong 2 mis-names the relevant cosmological-horizon variety (particle vs Hubble); prong 3 anchors to a WIN-053 framing that doesn\'t match WIN-053\'s actual argument; and the forced-dichotomy closing addresses prong 1's anchor (unified-theory entry) without closing prong 3's escape (sector decomposition). A defender attacking the paragraph for execution problems can do so without engaging the underlying structural argument — which is the worst outcome for an adversarial preemption.

deeper_analysis: The EXP-407 patch is structurally correct and lands the right shape of preemption. The defects are localized execution errors (Hole 1 = wrong technical term; Hole 2 = wrong cross-WIN citation; Hole 3 = unforeclosed escape from anticipated_objections that didn\'t propagate into the prose) that any working cosmologist or careful defender reading the paragraph will catch. Each is single-string-replaceable. The most important fix is Hole 1 (particle horizon → Hubble horizon) because cosmologists are the most-likely external reviewers of this paragraph and the term-confusion is the easiest paper-cut to land. Hole 2 (WIN-053 citation) is the most embarrassing because it asserts something WIN-053\'s own detail_evidence does NOT support; the same-parameter-double-duty critique is real and worth keeping, but the WIN-053 anchor needs to be the shared λ scale-length, not a non-existent 'hard magnetic cap'. Hole 3 (sector-decomposition escape) is the most strategically important because if landed, the prong 3 ALSO closes, and the closing forced-choice no longer needs to bear the structural load alone. Holes 4–6 (minor) can be folded into the same patch or deferred. The three stronger-argument additions (numerical-match-as-Hubble-import, self-defeat lever, inline EXP-407 anticipated-objection responses) would harden the paragraph to where defense_survives drops below 1 for any of the three retreat paths. RECOMMENDED CARRY-FORWARD CHECK: c8 should verify each of these has either been patched or deliberately deferred with rationale.

citations_verified: data/wins.json:WIN-029:detail_evidence — 9,086 km firmament height cited as conductive ceiling (3 hits on 'conductive ceiling', 2 hits on '9,086') — supports prong 3 anchor to WIN-029
data/wins.json:WIN-053:detail_evidence — references firmament_height once but as a non-derivational example, NOT as a hard magnetic cap. See Hole 2.
Cross-link anchors #win029 and #win053 in the inserted paragraph match the existing WIN ids in data/wins.json (id: '029' and id: '053' respectively).
citations_failed: Prong 3's characterization of firmament_height = 9,086 km as 'a hard magnetic-cap boundary in WIN-053' does not match WIN-053:detail_evidence which treats firmament_height only as a non-derivational example geometric property. The shared λ=8,619 km scale length IS the relevant cross-equation parameter in WIN-053. See Hole 2.
citations_unchecked: Dome-site assertion 'unified-theory table lists ρ_A = g₀ρ_bar/g† with status CONSISTENT and cites the RAR as its strongest cosmological confirmation' — this is the EXP-407 paraphrase of the dome's published unified-theory table. The phrase 'strongest cosmological confirmation' is also asserted in data/wins.json:WIN-052:detail_evidence (carried since at least c4). No fresh capture of the dome unified-theory table is in raw-text/ this cycle. Recommend a future capture into raw-text/ to anchor this citation. Severity: low — the claim has been in our review for multiple cycles without dome objection, and EXP-407 cited it as already-established context.

advocate_mode.best_defense: The strongest dome-defender rebuttal to the new paragraph specifically (not to the WIN as a whole) is the combination of (a) terminology-pedantry on 'particle horizon' (Hole 1), (b) WIN-053 mis-citation (Hole 2), and (c) sector-decomposition escape on prong 3 (Hole 3). A defender who lands all three can claim the entire preemption paragraph is execution-flawed even if the underlying structural argument is sound. Most-likely defender quote: 'Your three-pronged horizon-analogy preemption gets the cosmology term wrong (particle vs Hubble horizon), miscites your own WIN-053 (firmament_height is not a magnetic cap there), and forces a false dichotomy that ignores the sector-decomposition option you yourselves anticipated in EXP-407 but didn\'t bother to address in the published prose. Three execution errors in 343 words. The dome\'s horizon-analogy framing still stands.' defense_survives estimate: 1.5 — defender can claim a partial win on execution without engaging the underlying argument; the WIN-052 Self-Contradicted verdict survives because Holes 1–3 are localized prose problems not verdict-affecting. But the WIN's adversarial-preemption track record takes a credibility hit until the execution is cleaned up.
advocate_mode.preemptive_recommendation: Three single-string edits land all the moderate holes: (1) 'particle horizon' → 'Hubble horizon' (Hole 1); (2) 'as a hard magnetic-cap boundary in WIN-053' → 'as a co-opted scale-length in WIN-053\'s toroidal-ovoid magnetic field, where the same λ=8,619 km governs both firmament-height H(r) and magnetic-field B(r)' (Hole 2); (3) append to prong 3 final sentence: ' — and partitioning the parameter into separate electromagnetic and gravitational model sectors would require the dome to publish the two-sector derivation it does not have.' (Hole 3). All three are decider-self-applicable as M1 sweep. After landing, defense_survives drops from estimated 1.5 to ~0.7. The stronger-argument additions (numerical-match Hubble-import + self-defeat lever + inline EXP-407 anticipated-objection responses) are optional further hardening that would push defense_survives below 0.5 but require analyst expansion.

summary_for_decider: WIN-052.c7 reviews the EXP-407 horizon-analogy preemption paragraph (PROP-009: queue_id=367 set). Verdict holds Self-Contradicted. Three moderate execution-level holes in the new paragraph: (H1) 'particle horizon' should be 'Hubble horizon' — the recession-velocity-exceeds-c boundary is the Hubble horizon, not the particle horizon (which is defined by lookback to t=0). (H2) WIN-053 citation in prong 3 mis-anchors — WIN-053 does not treat firmament_height as a 'hard magnetic-cap boundary'; the relevant cross-equation parameter in WIN-053 is the shared λ=8,619 km scale length, not firmament_height. (H3) prong 3 forced-dichotomy doesn\'t foreclose the sector-decomposition escape that EXP-407\'s anticipated_objections[2] documented but didn\'t propagate into prose. Three minor holes: prong 1 ρ_A/λ_A elision, closing dichotomy missing reinterpretation third-option, WIN-029 cross-citation glossing the 9,086/8,537 internal inconsistency. All H1–H3 are single-string-replaceable; recommend decider M1 self-apply. Three stronger-argument enhancements (numerical-match-as-Hubble-import lever in prong 2; self-defeat lever; inline anticipated-objection responses) are optional analyst-expansion hardening. Class check: queue item was 'deep-attack' (singleton, correctly classified — three new lines of attack with no precursor in prior prose). No carry-forward holes from c6 — ISS-2026/2027/2028 all closed/fixed (verified in closed-issues.json; ISS-2038 closed wontfix per dup-of-ISS-2025). Recommended_action: minor_edit (three string replacements + optional enhancements). Anti-staleness check: clone HEAD cd9e9cc9 verified all six holes present in current detail_verdict_text — no parallel patches superseded any findings.
